document.source_title: Progress in reducing child deaths slows as 4.9 million children die before age five

claims.claim_text: 4.9 million children died before their fifth birthday in 2024.
claims.evidence_text: An estimated 4.9 million children died before their fifth birthday in 2024.

claims.claim_text: The pace of reduction in child mortality has slowed by more than 60 percent since 2015.
claims.evidence_text: However, since 2015, the pace of reduction in child mortality has slowed by more than 60 per cent.

claims.claim_text: Newborn deaths account for nearly half of all under-five deaths.
claims.evidence_text: Newborn deaths account for nearly half of all under-five deaths.

claims.claim_text: Malaria is the single largest killer in the under-five age group.
claims.evidence_text: Malaria remained the single largest killer in this age group (17 per cent).

claims.claim_text: Children born in fragile and conflict-affected countries are nearly three times more likely to die before their fifth birthday.
claims.evidence_text: Children born in these settings are nearly three times more likely to die before their fifth birthday than those elsewhere.

entities.name: UN Inter-agency Group for Child Mortality Estimation
entities.description: A group formed to share data on child mortality and improve estimation methods.
entities.evidence_text: The United Nations Inter-agency Group for Child Mortality Estimation or UN IGME was formed in 2004 to share data on child mortality.

entities.name: Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health
entities.description: An institution focused on public health research and education.
entities.evidence_text: The Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health works to keep millions safe from illness and injury by pioneering new research.

metrics.name: Child deaths
metrics.evidence_text: An estimated 4.9 million children died before their fifth birthday in 2024.

metrics.name: Newborn deaths
metrics.evidence_text: including 2.3 million newborns, according to new estimates released today.

metrics.name: Deaths from severe acute malnutrition
metrics.evidence_text: finding that more than 100,000 children aged 1-59 months – or 5 per cent – died from it in 2024.

metrics.name: Percentage of under-five deaths in sub-Saharan Africa
metrics.evidence_text: In 2024, sub-Saharan Africa accounted for 58 per cent of all under-five deaths.

citations.title: Progress in reducing child deaths slows as 4.9 million children die before age five

source_credibility.reason: The source is from the World Health Organization, a reputable international health agency.
